summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The InputFormat class is an abstract class that describes the input-specification for a Map-Reduce job.

CORE LOGIC:

The Map-Reduce framework relies on the InputFormat of the job to:

Validate the input-specification of the job.
Split-up the input file(s) into logical InputSplits, each of which is then assigned to an individual Mapper.
Provide the RecordReader implementation to be used to glean input records from the logical InputSplit for processing by the Mapper.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The InputFormat class has two abstract methods:

getSplits(JobContext context): This method is responsible for logically splitting the set of input files for the job and returning an array of InputSplits.
createRecordReader(InputSplit split, TaskAttemptContext context): This method is responsible for creating a RecordReader for a given split.

DEPENDENCIES:

The InputFormat class has no dependencies on other classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The RecordReader class is an abstract class that defines the interface for reading input data to the Mapper.

CORE LOGIC:
- The initialize() method is called once at the beginning of the task, and it is responsible for initializing the RecordReader with the input split and task context.
- The nextKeyValue() method is used to read the next key-value pair from the input data.
- The getCurrentKey() and getCurrentValue() methods are used to retrieve the current key and value that was read by the RecordReader.
- The getProgress() method is used to get the current progress of the RecordReader through its data.
- The close() method is used to close the RecordReader and release any resources it holds.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The RecordReader takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as input to the initialize() method.
- The nextKeyValue() method reads the next key-value pair from the input data and returns a boolean indicating whether a key-value pair was read.
- The getCurrentKey() and getCurrentValue() methods return the current key and value that was read by the RecordReader.
- The getProgress() method returns a float value between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating the fraction of the data read by the RecordReader.
- The close() method is used to close the RecordReader and release any resources it holds.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The RecordReader class implements the Closeable interface, which means it can be closed after use.
- The RecordReader class is used by the Mapper class to read input data and pass it to the map() method.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of the CombineFileInputFormat class is to create splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool.

CORE LOGIC:
The code creates splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool. It does this by iterating over all the files in the input set and processing them in a single iteration. For each file, it creates a OneFileInfo object that contains information about the file and its blocks. It then creates splits for all the blocks in the file, using the createSplits method.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a JobContext object as input, which contains the configuration and input paths. It returns a list of InputSplit objects, which represent the splits that will be used by the MapReduce job.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the FileInputFormat class and its methods, as well as the CombineFileSplit class and its methods. It also uses the CompressionCodecFactory class and its methods to check if a file is splittable.
chunk_summaries:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk is to create splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool.

CORE LOGIC:
The code creates splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool. It does this by iterating over all the files in the input set and processing them in a single iteration. For each file, it creates a OneFileInfo object that contains information about the file and its blocks. It then creates splits for all the blocks in the file, using the createSplits method.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a JobContext object as input, which contains the configuration and input paths. It returns a list of InputSplit objects, which represent the splits that will be used by the MapReduce job.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the FileInputFormat class and its methods, as well as the CombineFileSplit class and its methods. It also uses the CompressionCodecFactory class and its methods to check if a file is splittable.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code chunk is part of the CombineFileInputFormat class, which is used to split the input files into smaller splits based on a maximum size.

CORE LOGIC:
The code chunk iterates over a list of blocks and adds them to a validBlocks list. It also keeps track of the current split size and the list of racks that the blocks belong to. If the current split size exceeds the maximum size, it creates a new input split and adds it to the splits array.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code chunk takes in a list of blocks and a maximum size as input, and it outputs a list of input splits.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code chunk depends on the CombineFileSplit class, which is used to represent an input split. It also depends on the NetworkTopology class, which is used to determine the network topology of the hosts.

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- A generic RecordReader that can hand out different RecordReaders for each chunk in a CombineFileSplit.
- A CombineFileSplit can combine data chunks from multiple files.
- This class allows using different RecordReaders for processing these data chunks from different files.

CORE LOGIC:
- The code initializes the RecordReader for the next chunk in the CombineFileSplit.
- It gets a RecordReader for the idx-th chunk and sets up some helper config variables.
- It initializes the RecordReader for the first RecordReader by the MapTask.
- It is responsible for initializing subsequent RecordReaders.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The code takes a CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and Class as inputs.
- It returns a boolean value indicating whether there are more key-value pairs to read.
- It outputs the current key and value.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The code depends on the CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and RecordReader classes.
- It also depends on the Configuration class from the Hadoop framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class is a wrapper for a RecordReader that handles a single file split. It delegates most of the methods to the wrapped instance.

CORE LOGIC:
The constructor takes in a FileInputFormat, CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and an index. It creates a FileSplit based on the index and delegates the RecordReader creation to the input format. The initialize method checks if the file split is valid and initializes the delegate RecordReader. The nextKeyValue, getCurrentKey, getCurrentValue, getProgress, and close methods are delegated to the delegate RecordReader.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The constructor takes in a FileInputFormat, CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and an index. It creates a FileSplit based on the index and delegates the RecordReader creation to the input format. The initialize method takes in an InputSplit and TaskAttemptContext and initializes the delegate RecordReader. The nextKeyValue, getCurrentKey, getCurrentValue, getProgress, and close methods are delegated to the delegate RecordReader.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the FileInputFormat, CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and RecordReader classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class is an InputFormat that delegates behavior of paths to multiple other InputFormats.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class is to build a map of InputFormats to Paths, and then for each set of paths that have a common InputFormat and Mapper, add them to the same job and split together.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The class takes in a JobContext and returns a list of InputSplits.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the MultipleInputs class for getting the input format map and mapper type map. It also depends on the ReflectionUtils class for creating new instances of InputFormats and Mappers.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The main purpose of this chunk of code is to create a DelegatingRecordReader class that delegates the functionality to the underlying record reader in TaggedInputSplit.

CORE LOGIC:

The code uses ReflectionUtils to instantiate the InputFormat and create a RecordReader from the TaggedInputSplit. It then delegates the functionality to the original RecordReader.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as inputs and returns a DelegatingRecordReader as output.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the InputFormat, InputSplit, RecordReader, TaskAttemptContext, and ReflectionUtils classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The main purpose of this chunk of code is to define the FixedLengthInputFormat class, which is used to read input files containing fixed length records.

CORE LOGIC:

The code defines the FixedLengthInputFormat class, which extends the FileInputFormat class. It overrides the createRecordReader() method to return a FixedLengthRecordReader object, which is used to read the fixed length records from the input file.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The FixedLengthInputFormat class takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as input parameters in the createRecordReader() method. It returns a RecordReader object, which is used to read the fixed length records from the input file.

DEPENDENCIES:

The FixedLengthInputFormat class depends on the following classes:

FileInputFormat: This class provides the basic framework for reading input files.

CompressionCodecFactory: This class is used to determine the compression codec of a file.

FixedLengthRecordReader: This class is used to read the fixed length records from the input file.

The FixedLengthInputFormat class also depends on the following configuration properties:

FIXED_RECORD_LENGTH: This property is used to set the length of each record. It is set using the setRecordLength() method.

The FixedLengthInputFormat class also depends on the following methods:

isSplitable(): This method is used to determine whether a file is splitable or not. It returns true if the file is not compressed, and false otherwise.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- FixedLengthRecordReader is a RecordReader that reads fixed-length records from an input split.
- It returns the record offset as the key and the record as bytes in the value.

CORE LOGIC:
- The initialize method initializes the reader by setting the start and end positions of the split, and opening the input file.
- The nextKeyValue method reads the next record from the input stream and returns it as a key-value pair.
- The getProgress method calculates the progress of the reader by comparing the current position to the start and end positions of the split.
- The close method closes the input stream and returns the decompressor to the pool.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The initialize method takes a Configuration object, a split start position, a split length, and a Path object as input.
- The nextKeyValue method returns a LongWritable key and a BytesWritable value.
- The getProgress method returns a float value between 0 and 1 representing the progress of the reader.
- The close method does not take any input and does not return any output.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The FixedLengthRecordReader class depends on the Configuration, Path, FSDataInputStream, Seekable, CompressionCodec, CompressionInputStream, and Decompressor classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class treats a line in the input as a key/value pair separated by a separator character.

CORE LOGIC:

The separator can be specified in the config file under the attribute name mapreduce.input.keyvaluelinerecordreader.key.value.separator. The default separator is the tab character ('\t').

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The input is a line in the input. The output is a key/value pair.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the LineRecordReader class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- This class is an InputFormat for reading plain text files.
- It breaks the files into lines and divides each line into key and value parts.
- If no separator byte exists, the key will be the entire line and the value will be empty.

CORE LOGIC:
- The createRecordReader method creates a KeyValueLineRecordReader for reading the input split.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The input is a generic InputSplit and the output is a KeyValueLineRecordReader.

DEPENDENCIES:
- This class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.input package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

- Treats keys as offset in file and value as line.
- Reads one extra line, which lies outside the upper split limit.

CORE LOGIC:

- Initializes the file and seek to the start of the split.
- Skips UTF-8 BOM if present.
- Reads lines and updates position.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

- Input: InputSplit, TaskAttemptContext
- Output: LongWritable, Text

DEPENDENCIES:

- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.input.LineRecordReader
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.InputSplit
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext
- org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.FSDataInputStream
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.FutureDataInputStreamBuilder
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.Seekable
- org.apache.hadoop.io.LongWritable
- org.apache.hadoop.io.Text
- org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.CodecPool
- org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.CompressionCodec
- org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.SplitCompressionInputStream
- org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.SplittableCompressionCodec
- org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.CompressionCodecFactory
- org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.Decompressor
- org.apache.hadoop.util.functional.FutureIO
- org.slf4j.Logger
- org.slf4j.LoggerFactory

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The NLineInputFormat class is a FileInputFormat that splits N lines of input as one split.

CORE LOGIC:

The getSplits method splits the input files into splits based on the number of lines per split.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The createRecordReader method returns a LineRecordReader, which reads and consumes at least one character out of its upper split boundary.

DEPENDENCIES:

The NLineInputFormat class depends on the LineRecordReader class, which is used to read and consume the input data.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class converts the input keys and values to their String forms by calling toString() method.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class is to read key/value pairs from a SequenceFile and convert the keys and values to their String forms using the toString() method.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The input to this class is an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext. The output is a key-value pair where the key and value are both of type Text.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the SequenceFileRecordReader class for reading key/value pairs from a SequenceFile.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This chunk of code is an input format for sequence files.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this chunk is to create a record reader for the input split and task attempt context.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The inputs to this chunk are an input split and a task attempt context. The output is a record reader.

DEPENDENCIES:
The dependencies of this chunk include the SequenceFileRecordReader class, which is used to create the record reader. It also depends on the FileInputFormat class, which provides the framework for reading files.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- This class is a RecordReader for SequenceFile.
- It reads key-value pairs from a SequenceFile.

CORE LOGIC:
- It initializes the SequenceFile.Reader and sets the start and end positions for reading.
- It reads the next key-value pair from the SequenceFile.
- It calculates the progress within the input split.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The input is a SequenceFile.
- The output is the key-value pairs read from the SequenceFile.

DEPENDENCIES:
- This class depends on the SequenceFile.Reader and Configuration classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The main purpose of this chunk is to define the TextInputFormat class, which extends the FileInputFormat class.
- It is used to read plain text files and break them into lines.

CORE LOGIC:
- The createRecordReader method is overridden to return a LineRecordReader, which is used to read the lines from the input file.
- The isSplitable method is overridden to determine if the input file is splittable based on its compression codec.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The createRecordReader method takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as input and returns a RecordReader.
- The isSplitable method takes a JobContext and a Path as input and returns a boolean value.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The TextInputFormat class depends on the LineRecordReader class, which is used to read the lines from the input file.
- It also depends on the CompressionCodecFactory class, which is used to determine the compression codec of the input file.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class is a refinement of InputFormat that requires implementors to provide a ComposableRecordReader instead of a RecordReader.

CORE LOGIC:

The createRecordReader method is the key algorithm in this class. It takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as parameters and returns a ComposableRecordReader.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The createRecordReader method takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as parameters and returns a ComposableRecordReader.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the ComposableRecordReader class, which is a key collaborator class. It also depends on the WritableComparable and Writable classes, which are imported.
